events.title: Screen Time with Toronto Public Health
events.description: In this session, the benefits and impacts of screen time will be explored. Discussion will include looking at recommendations for screen time per age of the child (between 0 and 6 years of age) and how to model and manage appropriate screen time behaviour. Physical activity recommendations as an alternative to screen time will be reviewed.

events.title: St. Lawrence Library Relocation and Expansion (Children's Services & Programming Focus Group)
events.description: Toronto Public Library is preparing plans to relocate and expand the St. Lawrence Branch. The new district branch at 339 Queen Street East will offer expanded space and services - and we’d like your input. Share your ideas on the features and services you’d like to see in the new location. This session will explore children’s services and programming for the new branch.

events.description: Participants will learn about the hidden rivers in Toronto and explore how our changing cityscape impacts on our waterways and ecosystems. Led by Shannyn Porter and Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Solar System Stories: A Merril Collection Exhibit
events.description: Take a trip around our solar system without leaving the comfort of planet Earth. Explore the strange landscapes of the inner and outer planets and their moons. We've even included Pluto! Learn how our understanding of these worlds has evolved since their discovery. In our exhibit you'll find original art, novels, short stories and more set on these fantastic celestial bodies. This exhibit can be viewed during the Merril Collection's regular hours. It opens on Tuesday, May 19 following the holida

events.title: Month long Scavenger Hunt: Follow the rainbowsaurus trail!
events.description: Jones Branch would like to invite you on a fun and magical quest to find the legendary Rainbowsaurus and all 11 of his colourful friends! Once you found all 12 animals on your scavenger hunt sheet, you will earn a certificate! For school-aged children and their caregivers. Drop-in.

events.title: Branch Art Exhibit: Meditative Art by Izabella Lipowski
events.description: Throughout the month of June the works of Izabella Lipowski are on display at Morningside branch Izabella is a self - taught artist and retired Community Support Worker who was lucky to merge her formal education with her love of art. During her working years she taught various crafts (weaving, paper work, jewelry making) as well as drawing and painting in different mediums to adults with special needs, encouraging their creativity. Her favourite subject and biggest inspiration is draw

events.title: Toys Alive! Exhibition Tour
events.description: Curious about what your toys get up to when you are not looking? We jumped into the toy box to discover just that. From dolly’s adventures to teddy bears on the search for honey to toy soldiers on patrol. Join us for a free guided tour of TD Gallery’s current exhibition Toys Alive! Explore literary works from the late 1800s to present day from the Osborne Collection of Early Children’s Books! Registration is not required but is encouraged. Please meet at the entrance to the TD Gallery.  Inf

events.title: Snap Circuits for Kids
events.description: For ages 8-12. Light up your imagination! In this hands-on workshop, young engineers will explore the basics of electricity and electronics using Snap Circuits. We'll learn how to build simple circuits to power lights, make sounds, and more. Participants will work in pairs to construct projects from the Snap Circuits blueprints. No prior experience is needed—just bring your curiosity! Drop in. No registration required, but space is limited.

Its goal is to provide the animal team, made up of an assortment of rescued pets, with a permanent home and the best possible care while sharing a love for animals with others. All Pawsitively Pets programs focus on responsible pet ownership and the lifelong commitment of owning a pet. For ages 4-12.  Children ages 5 and under must be accompanied with a caregiver/guardian. events.title: Explorum Workshop: Kaléidescope / Kaleidescope (Drop-in)
events.description: Qu’est-ce qu’un kaléidoscope ? Une illusion d’optique ? De la magie ? De l’art ? En fait c’est un peu de tout ça. C’est un instrument développé il y a plus de 200 ans qui utilise la réflexion de la lumière sur des miroirs pour créer des motifs répétitifs envoutants. Dans cet atelier, les enfants feront leur propre kaléidoscope et seront exposés aux termes de réflexion et réfraction de lumière tout en étant captivés par la physique des scintillements des motifs sur les surfaces réfléchissantes. events.description: Join us for a fun and engaging storytime followed by a hands-on craft activity perfect for children ages 3-6 with their parents or caregivers. Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: Magic Mel's Amazing Magic Show
events.description: Find your "inner magic" with The Amazing Magic Mel during this upbeat, hilarious and interactive magical experience. Expect a healthy dose of participation and self-empowerment as she leads aspiring magicians of all ages on a journey to become "official magicians" during this fast-paced, comedy magic show for the entire family. Suitable for children 3 years old and up with their parent or caregiver. This program can hold up to 60 guests including adults with their children. When signing up pleas

events.title: BenTastic Magic Show
events.description: Find your "inner magic" during this upbeat, hilarious, and interactive magical experience with BenTastic! Expect a healthy dose of participation and self-empowerment as he leads aspiring magicians of all ages on a journey to become "real magicians" during this fast-paced, fun and amazing magic show! For ages 0-12.

events.title: Lego Club
events.description: Every Wednesday come out to build, create and use your imagination with LEGO. Enjoy a new building challenge each week. Complete 5 challenges and get a sticker! Drop-in.

events.title: Summer Lego Challenge
events.description: Looking for an afternoon challenge? Come out to our Summer Lego Challenge for hands on learning about design and engineering, while building towers, bridges, boats and more! For ages 6-12.

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Learn Not to Burn: Fire Safety for Children
events.description: Join the Toronto Fire Service in this special visit to teach our young community members about fire safety and prevention, and what to do when there is an emergency. There might even be a special visit from a Fire Engine! Drop-in program for children and families. No registration required, space is limited.

events.title: Summer BINGO
events.description: For children ages 6-12. Cool off and play a few rounds of Summer Bingo for fun and the chance to win cool books, stickers and more. No registration – but ticket is required. Space is limited. Tickets will be handed out at the 2 nd floor desk 15 minutes before the program starts.

events.title: Fictional Character Bingo and TD Summer Reading Club Draw
events.description: Let's finish off this summer with a fun round of Bingo! This time, we'll be doing popular fictional characters in lieu of numbers. Have you read lots of stories? Maybe you can guess who they are too! Prizes will be given out for Bingo winners! Also, if you've completed the TD Summer Reading Club challenge of finishing 12 books, we'll be doing a draw for special prizes at the end of the program. Drop-in!

events.title: Yoga Storytime with Appleseed Yoga
events.description: ​Join us for an interactive, movement-based program where we read stories and make them come alive with our bodies! Appleseed Yoga for Kids will deliver inspiring stories that get the whole family moving. Class begins and ends with song and breathwork and is filled with pages of creativity and activity. Kids and families can sit quietly and listen, do partial poses or make up their own, following our lead. It's ALL yoga! No experience, mats, or materials required. Drop-in with limited space. All

events.title: Wonder Workshop - Pollution Challenge
events.description: Do you ever think about the impact of one little person or one tiny thing on the planet? When one pollutant becomes many, the planet hurts. But when one person becomes many, positive change can happen. In this workshop, you will design and build your own creature or machine that will help keep our precious planet clean! Plus, enter a prize draw for a chance to win an awesome book.  For kids ages 4 to 8. Space is limited. Registration begins June 1st. Information Collection Notice: The perso

events.title: Chess Club

events.description: This month we will play with electricity and circuitry. Parent/Caregiver must attend. events.title: Fizzy Science Lab: Bath Bomb Creations
events.description: Get ready to mix, mold, and fizz. In this hands-on program, kids will become junior scientists as they explore the chemistry behind bath bombs. Through simple experiments and creative crafting, participants will learn how ingredients react to create those exciting bubbles and pops—then make their own bath bomb to take home. Ages 6-12. Registration Required. Information Collection Notice: The personal information on this form is collected by the Toronto Public Library under the Public Libraries A

events.description: LEGO Free Play is a fun, creative program where children can build anything they imagine using LEGO bricks. There are no instructions or rules to follow, kids are free to explore, create, and play at their own pace. This open-ended play encourages creativity, problem-solving, and social interaction. Drop-in program, no registration required.

events.title: Saturday Chess Club
events.description: Join us on the 2nd floor every Saturday for a few games of chess in a welcoming environment. All ages and all levels are welcome. Children must be accompanied by a parent or guardian to participate. Any unsupervised individuals under 18 will not be able to participate in the program. This is a drop-in program. Please note that there will be no chess club on Saturday May 16 for our Big Book Sale.

events.title: Robotics Workshops for Youth
events.description: This program is for kids and youth in grades 3-12. Join Upper Toronto Community Robotics for accessible, hands-on robotics and engineering workshops for youth! These programs will help you build foundational skills in engineering design, safety, CAD software, 3D modeling, and introductory robotics coding. This is a drop-in program.

events.title: Movie Monday: Dog Man (2025)
events.description: Join us to watch the film and enjoy making a themed craft or colouring. Based on the beloved book series by Dav Pilkey, Dog Man follows the adventures of a half-dog, half-man crimefighter who is sworn to protect and serve. As he doggedly pursues the mischievous feline supervillain, Petey the Cat, chaos, laughter, and heroic antics ensue in this fun-filled animated adventure for the whole family. Age Rating: PG Run Time: 1h 29m For Families. Drop in.

events.description: Participants will learn about the hidden rivers in Toronto and explore how our changing cityscape impacts on our waterways and ecosystems. Led by Shannyn Porter and Ursula Handleigh

events.description: Discover the science behind things that make you go...ewww! Make "snot" and sneeze it while exploring viscosity, identify various animals from their scat (i.e. poop) and learn about digestion while producing a burp in a bag. Join us on a gross but fun-filled science adventure!

events.title: Solar System Stories: A Merril Collection Exhibit
events.description: Take a trip around our solar system without leaving the comfort of planet Earth. Explore the strange landscapes of the inner and outer planets and their moons. We've even included Pluto! Learn how our understanding of these worlds has evolved since their discovery. In our exhibit you'll find original art, novels, short stories and more set on these fantastic celestial bodies. This exhibit can be viewed during the Merril Collection's regular hours. It opens on Tuesday, May 19 following the holida

events.title: Branch Art Exhibit: Meditative Art by Izabella Lipowski
events.description: Throughout the month of June the works of Izabella Lipowski are on display at Morningside branch Izabella is a self - taught artist and retired Community Support Worker who was lucky to merge her formal education with her love of art. During her working years she taught various crafts (weaving, paper work, jewelry making) as well as drawing and painting in different mediums to adults with special needs, encouraging their creativity. Her favourite subject and biggest inspiration is draw

events.title: Branch Art Exhibit: When The Cavas Speaks-Artistic Journeys by Sarah Chi
events.description: Throughout the month of June, works by artist Sarah Chi are on display at Northern District Library branch in an exhibit. Sarah is her school’s Art Club Head, a TPL Teen Council and Young Voices Council member. She has created art since age six and began expressing personal ideas at fourteen. Working in oil, acrylics, ink, printmaking, and mixed media, she has won Scholastic Art & Writing Awards (Gold and Silver Key) and had work published in Young Voices magazine. She co-founded

events.title: Tech History Trivia Night
events.description: Join us for an exciting night of trivia that’ll test your tech-based knowledge, with fun subcategories for a total of 5 rounds. Upgrade your knowledge with a bit friendly competition!

events.title: Toys Alive! Exhibition Tour
events.description: Curious about what your toys get up to when you are not looking? We jumped into the toy box to discover just that. From dolly’s adventures to teddy bears on the search for honey to toy soldiers on patrol. Join us for a free guided tour of TD Gallery’s current exhibition Toys Alive! Explore literary works from the late 1800s to present day from the Osborne Collection of Early Children’s Books! Registration is not required but is encouraged. Please meet at the entrance to the TD Gallery.  Inf

events.title: Game On!

Dive into some casual play or friendly competition with popular video games for Switch and PS4/5, or enjoy some unplugged fun with board games when you need a screen break.

events.title: Youth Hub: Tournament Tuesday
events.description: A weekly recreational program where youth compete in friendly games, challenges, and tournaments that build teamwork, confidence, leadership, and community connection in a fun and supportive environment. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Flying Butterfly Bookmark
events.description: Design your own flying butterfly bookmark and discover the science behind propellers and motion! Teens will decorate the butterfly wings, wind up the mechanism, place it inside a book, and watch it fly out when the book opens. A fun hands-on STEM activity that mixes creativity, engineering, and surprise. All materials provided. Limited supplies available. This is an in person drop-in program for teens 13 -17. events.title: Music Copyright and Ethics in the AI Era
events.description: When you use AI to create music, who actually owns it? Can a melody generated by a machine infringe on someone else's copyright? events.description: Express yourself visually and connect with your creative side in this art journaling workshop for teens! Art journaling combines traditional journaling with visual art, using sketching, paints, collage, and more. Artist Anna Redish will guide participants with prompts to create unique journal pages. This will be a supportive, judgment-free zone, where participants are encouraged to create their art however feels right to them.

events.title: Boba Drink Making Workshop
events.description: Chef Yumiko leads a hands-on workshop to make the popular Asian beverage, Brown Sugar Milk with Boba. Participants will learn to prepare and use the classic ingredients​ including milk, molasses and tapioca pearls to make this visually pleasing and sweet drink. Chef Yumiko will also provide a brief history of Boba and why it's so popular globally.

events.title: Before the Blue Jays: Professional Baseball in Toronto Prior to 1977

Learn about the major events, venues, and people who trail-blazed the way to the arrival of Major League Baseball in Toronto.

events.title: Beginner's Guide to Making Comics
events.description: Have you ever wanted to create your own comic, manga, or anime-inspired story? In this interactive workshop, teens will learn the basics of comic creation through hands-on activities, creative storytelling, and guided drawing exercises. Participants will explore comic and manga techniques including panels, page layouts, speech bubbles, sound effects, character design, and visual storytelling. They’ll also learn how to build engaging characters and create their own one-page comic or manga scene.

Space is limited. Please arrive at 3:30pm to secure your spot in the program. Participants must be available both dates to ensure completion of project.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutrit

events.title: Youth Hub: Mindful Mondays
events.description: Every Monday, enjoy a series of stress-relieving mindfulness activities, crafts, and games to help ease anxiety and give you a moment of Zen. Enjoy mindful colouring sheets, rainbow scratch art, mindfulness card decks, gratitude journals, and more. All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by

events.title: The Hidden Rivers
events.description: Participants will learn about the hidden rivers in Toronto and explore how our changing cityscape impacts on our waterways and ecosystems. Looking at archival images and current references, participants will be guided to bring their own stories into our city’s images using material such as watercolours, crayons, playdough, text, etc onto colouring pages. Led by Shannyn Porter and Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Youth Hub: Board Game Café
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Tuesday to play board games with your friends! Ask us for our list of board games, and check our monthly calendar for Tournaments with prizes! All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Study Hall Wednesdays
events.description: On Wednesdays, the Eatonville Youth Hub becomes a quiet space for teens to study, work on projects, and request homework help and resume help from staff. Bring your homework and enjoy a snack in a space that is just for teens. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Youth Hub: Crafty Thursdays
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Thursday for a series of crafting activities like painting, clay sculpting, sewing and embroidery, resin art, jewelry making, perler beads, block printing, and more! Check tpl.ca or our monthly calendar for the activity of the week. All materials provided. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily.

events.title: Soundscape
events.description: Next we will be inspired by the library’s architecture and happenings as we are guided by musician Aniqa Qadir to create a soundscape. Working in groups, visitors will be prompted to absorb the sounds of the building and led through the process of creating an audio journey. This playful session welcomes participants to break the whisper norm of a library and interrupt the space with their observations. Led by Aniqa Qadir and Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Taxation and Government Benefits
events.description: Presented by Riverdale Immigrant Women’s Centre (RIWC) , this workshop will provide guidance on tax filing, understanding available benefits and credits and its eligibility requirements, and how to access applicable government financial supports. Registration encouraged . Drop-ins are welcome until capacity is reached.

events.title: Cercle de Conversation Française / French Conversation Circle
events.description: Nous vous invitons à pratiquer votre français dans un environnement informel ! Tous les niveaux sont bienvenus. We invite you to practice your French in an informal environment! This French Conversation Circle is open to all levels. Accessibilité: Le sous-titrage en direct ou automatique, l’interprétation en langue des signes, les services d’intervenant, ou les services d’accompagnement sont disponibles sur demande. Veuillez soumettre votre demande au moins deux semaines à l’avance à : tpl.ca/ac

events.title: Book Club
events.description: Come and join us on the third Thursday of every other month to discuss a variety of books. Everyone is welcome! Drop in, no registration required. This year we are reading:  February 19 -  The Strangers by Katherena Vermette April 16 -  Big Little Lies by Liane Moriarty June 18 -  On the Ravine by Vincent Lam September 17 -  Tomorrow, and Tomorrow, and Tomorrow by Gabrielle Zevin November 19 -  Small Things Like These by

events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Friday to play video games on our Nintendo Switch or PS5 with your friends! Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Robotics Workshops for Youth
events.description: This program is for kids and youth in grades 3-12. Join Upper Toronto Community Robotics for accessible, hands-on robotics and engineering workshops for youth! These programs will help you build foundational skills in engineering design, safety, CAD software, 3D modeling, and introductory robotics coding. This is a drop-in program.

events.title: NatureTO: Trees Around the Branch Tour
events.description: The Brookbanks branch and its neighbourhood is home to a variety of trees. Join the City of Toronto Urban Forestry team to explore the natural world on a local neighbourhood tour. Learn how to recognize different species and fun facts about their cultural and ecological importance. Free program.  Registration will open up one month before the program date. Children under 12 must be accompanied by a parent/caregiver. events.title: Retirement Planning
events.description: Presented by Riverdale Immigrant Women’s Centre (RIWC) , this workshop is an introduction to long-term financial planning including retirement savings and better understanding of federal income benefits, such as, Canada Pension Plan (CPP) and Old Age Security (OAS). Registration encouraged . Drop-ins are welcome until capacity is reached.

Bring your homework and enjoy a snack in a space that is just for teens. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Youth Hub: Crafty Thursdays
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Thursday for a series of crafting activities like painting, clay sculpting, sewing and embroidery, resin art, jewelry making, perler beads, block printing, and more! Check tpl.ca or our monthly calendar for the activity of the week. All materials provided. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily.

events.title: Youth Hub: Video Game Café
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Friday to play video games on our Nintendo Switch or PS5 with your friends! Ask us for our list of video games, and check our monthly calendar for Gaming Tournaments with prizes! This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Sunday Family Game Time
events.description: Join us for a family games afternoon from Lego to Uno. We will also have some colouring and crafts for everyone Every Sunday afternoon all ages welcome

events.title: Sunday Family Club
events.description: Enjoy and have fun on Sunday afternoons at Guildwood Library! Will you colour your favourite characters, do a craft or show off your building skills with LEGO and KEVA? Maybe it will be Snap Circuits or Board Games? Every Sunday come in for a surprise!

events.title: Youth Hub: Mindful Mondays
events.description: Every Monday, enjoy a series of stress-relieving mindfulness activities, crafts, and games to help ease anxiety and give you a moment of Zen. Enjoy mindful colouring sheets, rainbow scratch art, mindfulness card decks, gratitude journals, and more. All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. events.title: Youth Hub: Board Game Café
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Tuesday to play board games with your friends! Ask us for our list of board games, and check our monthly calendar for Tournaments with prizes! All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service.
